is there some TUI to configure the models that we use for tenex-edge?

No, there isn't — providers.json/llms.json under ~/.tenex-edge are edited by hand, and the only TUI in the codebase is the session-spawner picker. Offered to sketch a `tenex-edge models` TUI if desired.

use inquirer crate -- make it called `tenex-edge config` -- one of the menu options is configuring providers, another is configuring models (with model listing from the different providers (i.e. list the models from the configured ollama endpoint (somtimes localhost, could be anything, could be sometimes ollama cloud, etc) -- or the openrouter models -- with fuzzy search, etc a REAL good UX while you start working on the basics launch a fable agent to give you the UX/UI (including colors, etc) so we don't make something shitty

Built and verified `tenex-edge config` with provider and model menus, live model fetching with fuzzy search, secret masking, and a Fable-agent-derived UX spec. Full test suite and clippy are clean; deferred lower-priority polish items (OSC-11 theme auto-detection and was:<old value> diff line).

Noted the Fable agent's summary matches what was already incorporated from its spec. Asked the user whether to implement the remaining polish items (OSC-11 auto-detection and the was:<old value> diff line) or leave the build as-is.
